mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment rate signals labor market tightening and rising consumer confidence, which drives increased discretionary spending and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 10.2% in Oct 2009 suggests recovery phase beginning
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with labor market stabilization
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces positive 20d returns in fewer than 55% of instances where UNRATE declines month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) directly indicates consumer spending strength and aggregate demand expansion, which translates to higher corporate revenues and equity valuations within a 5-trading-day window as market reprices growth expectations upward.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 344.7B in Sep 2009 reflects early recovery
failure_condition: When RSAFS rises > 2% MoM, SPX produces positive 5d returns in fewer than 58% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.01 over rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate near zero combined with declining inflation (CPIAUCSL growth slowing) signals sustained monetary accommodation, reducing real discount rates and supporting equity valuations over a 60-trading-day horizon as liquidity remains abundant.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.12% and CPIAUCSL at 215.8 in Oct 2009
failure_condition: When both conditions are met, SPX produces positive 60d returns in fewer than 62% of instances, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling 2-year regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) expansion signals improving capacity utilization and manufacturing strength, which precedes earnings growth and equity price appreciation over 20 trading days as supply-side momentum builds.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 98.46 in Sep 2009 showing recovery from crisis lows
failure_condition: When INDPRO rises > 1.5% MoM, SPX produces positive 20d returns in fewer than 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly signal broad-based demand recovery across residential and consumer sectors, creating a multiplier effect on corporate earnings that drives positive equity returns over 20 trading days when both indicators accelerate simultaneously.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises > 5% MoM AND RSAFS rises > 1.5% MoM
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 590K and RSAFS at 344.7B in Sep 2009
regime_hint: synchronized demand recovery across housing and consumption
failure_condition: When both conditions are met, SPX produces positive 20d returns in fewer than 65% of instances, or the interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling 2-year regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth accelerates above 0.3% month-over-month, signaling labor market momentum that reduces recession risk and increases consumer confidence, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as the expansion becomes self-reinforcing.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 130.8M in Oct 2009, stabilizing after crisis
regime_hint: labor market stabilization and early expansion
failure_condition: When PAYEMS rises > 0.3% MoM, SPX produces positive 60d returns in fewer than 63% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) acceleration signals strengthening aggregate demand and corporate profit expansion, which drives equity valuations higher over a 60-trading-day horizon as earnings forecasts are revised upward in response to confirmed demand strength.
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13014B in Q2 2009, beginning recovery from recession
failure_condition: When QoQ annualized growth exceeds 1.5%, SPX produces positive 60d returns in fewer than 64% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains subdued while unemployment (UNRATE) is elevated, creating a regime where central banks maintain accommodative policy and equity risk premiums compress, driving positive 5-trading-day returns as investors rotate into equities seeking yield.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 10.2% and CPIAUCSL at 215.8 in Oct 2009
regime_hint: slack-driven monetary accommodation with low inflation
failure_condition: When both conditions are met, SPX produces positive 5d returns in fewer than 58% of instances, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling 1-year regressions.
